answers.value:
Please increase the aggregate annual overseas medical and evacuation limit from AUD 5,000,000 to AUD 7,500,000 to reflect increased staff travel to higher-cost medical jurisdictions in Europe and North America. We would also like to:

1. Add coverage for up to 10 days of combined business and leisure per trip (currently limited to 5 days), as a number of senior engineers are extending their stays to include weekends and short holidays after project work.
2. Include newly hired graduate engineers and project coordinators under the definition of insured persons (currently only employees at supervisor level and above are automatically covered). Our total headcount has increased from 85 to 112 in the last 12 months, with approximately 40 staff now travelling on behalf of the company.
3. Extend cover to include incidental manual work at client sites (e.g. supervising equipment installation, on-site testing and commissioning, and occasional work in light industrial environments). We are not involved in mining, offshore work, or heavy construction, but our engineers increasingly attend factory floors and logistics hubs for equipment commissioning.
4. Maintain all other existing sums insured and benefits as per the current schedule, including baggage, money, rental vehicle excess, and personal accident benefits.

If any of the above requires separate endorsement, please advise the additional premium and any specific conditions or exclusions that may apply.

answers.value: All business travel is conducted on regular commercial airlines (Qantas, Virgin Australia, Singapore Airlines, Emirates, etc.) and on scheduled rail or road services. We do not currently use charter aircraft or helicopters, and we do not anticipate doing so over the next 12 months. The largest group trips are typically for industry conferences (e.g. National Manufacturing Week, engineering symposiums) where 6–8 staff may be on the same flight and staying at the same accommodation. Travel is coordinated centrally by our office manager using a corporate travel management portal, and all travellers are required to follow our internal travel risk management and safety policies, including pre-trip briefings for higher-risk destinations and mandatory registration of itineraries in our internal system.
